title: Email Credentials
summary: Connect the inbox directly (alternative to forwarding) and save credentials so BuildVision can open documents behind protected links.
overview:
Two email-side credentials round out ingestion:

1. **Inbox connector** — an alternative to forwarding. Authorize BuildVision to read the bid-desk inbox and new bids land automatically. Use this *or* forwarding, not both.
2. **Saved credentials** — so BuildVision can retrieve documents behind protected links (SharePoint, Google Drive, OneDrive) without anyone manually downloading and re-uploading.

> CRM sync now lives in its own **CRM Integration** section.
prerequisites: Decision made: forward the inbox (Email Forwarding) or connect it (this section)
tips: Connecting the inbox and forwarding are interchangeable paths — choose one per client.
Saved credentials are **per user**, not per org — each rep who receives protected links adds their own under User Settings → Saved Credentials.
One credential per platform covers all links of that type the account can access.
warnings: Never store credentials that don't belong to the client's own account.
Setting up both forwarding and the inbox connector causes duplicate ingestion — pick one.
commonMistakes: Enabling both forwarding and the inbox connector, causing duplicate bids.
Expecting protected links to resolve without any saved credential on file.
Adding a credential under the wrong user (they are per user, not shared).

steps.title: Connect the inbox (if not forwarding)
steps.description: Click **Connect your email** and authorize BuildVision to read bid-related emails and attachments. New bids then land automatically. Skip this if the client is using Email Forwarding.
steps.verify: Authorization succeeds; a test bid lands automatically.

steps.title: Set up saved credentials for protected links
steps.description: For clients whose bids link to protected SharePoint / Google Drive / OneDrive documents, add credentials under **User Settings → Saved Credentials → + Add Credential**. Choose the provider, name it, enter the client's own username/password, and Create.
steps.verify: A protected test link resolves and its document is retrieved automatically.

callouts.text: Credentials belong to the user account, not the org. Each person receiving protected links adds their own.

screenshots.caption: User Settings → Saved Credentials — add one credential per platform via “+ Add Credential”.
Add Credential — pick a provider (Microsoft, OneDrive, SharePoint, Google…), name it, enter the login, and Create.
